--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTCBDXJKQA68WPEE4MJW77ZH
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9880'
+title: Migrate code-context-cli to schema-driven op commands
+---
+## What
+Replace the hand-written operation subcommand enums in code-context-cli with schema-driven generation from the code_context tool's FULL schema (via card B's shared generator), eliminating the parallel hand-maintained command tree that can drift from the actual operations.
+
+Today: `apps/code-context-cli/src/cli.rs` hand-declares `Commands` (the op groups `Get`/`Search`/`List`/`Grep`/`Query`/`Find`/`Rebuild`/`Clear`/`Lsp`/`Detect` at :87-137) and a sub-enum per group (`GetCommands` at :173, etc.), and `apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/ops.rs::run_operation` (:403) matches those variants to build the JSON args object then calls `CodeContextTool::execute` (:439).
+
+Two constraints to respect:
+1. `cli.rs` is compiled standalone by `build.rs` via `#[path = "src/cli.rs"]` for docs/manpages/completions and may only depend on `clap` + `std` (see the file header). So the schema-driven runtime tree must be built in `main.rs`/a runtime module, NOT inside `cli.rs`. Mirror kanban-cli's split: keep a static lifecycle `cli.rs` (Serve/Init/Deinit/Doctor/Skill/Completion) for build.rs, and build the op subcommands at runtime from the schema (kanban-cli does exactly this in `main.rs::build_cli`).
+2. Preserve the dispatch path: `CodeContextTool::execute` is still the target. The hand-written per-variant arg mapping in `ops.rs::run_operation` is replaced by `swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen::extract_noun_verb_arguments` producing the `{ "op": ..., ...args }` object, which `execute` consumes.
+
+Changes:
+- `apps/code-context-cli/Cargo.toml`: add `swissarmyhammer-operations` dep (currently absent; it depends on `swissarmyhammer-tools` which re-exports operations — prefer a direct dep on `swissarmyhammer-operations` for `cli_gen`).
+- `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs`: build the op command tree at runtime from `CodeContextTool` full schema (`tool.operations()` → `_full` generator → `build_commands_from_schema`); route non-lifecycle matches through `extract_noun_verb_arguments` → `execute`.
+- Remove the op-group enums from `cli.rs` (`Get`/`Search`/.../`Detect` and their `*Commands` sub-enums) and the per-variant mapping in `commands/ops.rs`, keeping the lifecycle commands.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] code-context op commands are generated from the schema; the hand-written `*Commands` op enums are gone from `cli.rs`.
+- [ ] The generated command tree covers every code_context operation (get symbol/callgraph/blastradius/status, search symbol/code/workspace_symbol, list symbols, grep code, query ast, find duplicates, rebuild index, clear status, lsp status, detect projects, etc.).
+- [ ] Lifecycle commands (serve/init/deinit/doctor/skill/completion) still work and build.rs doc/manpage/completion generation still compiles.
+- [ ] Existing dispatch still works: a generated op invocation reaches `CodeContextTool::execute` and returns output.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Adapt the existing `run_operation` integration tests in `apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/ops.rs` (:918+, e.g. `test_run_operation_get_status`, `_json`) to drive through the schema-built command tree + `extract_noun_verb_arguments`, asserting the same end-to-end result (creation → execute → text/JSON → exit code).
+- [ ] Add a command-tree coverage test asserting the generated nouns/verbs match `CodeContextTool::operations()` op strings.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p code-context-cli` passes.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the command-tree coverage test + adapt the run_operation tests first, then build the runtime schema-driven tree and delete the hand-written enums.
\ No newline at end of file

+- [x] `cargo nextest run -p code-context-cli` passes (64 passed, 2 skipped embedding-gated).
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-07 15:58)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/ops.rs:50` — run_operation has two adjacent bool parameters (json_output, no_progress); at call sites the literals are unreadable and trivially swappable, with no compiler protection against transposing them. Replace the two bools with explicit enums, e.g. `OutputMode { Json, Text }` and `Progress { Shown, Suppressed }`, so calls read `run_operation(args, OutputMode::Text, Progress::Suppressed)`.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:119` — The lifecycle subcommand builders (serve/init/deinit/doctor) reimplement the same builders already present in kanban-cli and shelltool-cli, differing only in the about-string; the whole family is now triplicated. Provide shared builders parameterized by binary name + about text (e.g. `serve_subcommand(about)`, `lifecycle_subcommands(spec)`) in the shared scaffold crate; each CLI passes only its strings.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:160` — completion_subcommand duplicates the entire multi-line bash/zsh/fish/powershell long_about help block verbatim across three binaries plus dynamic_cli; one wording fix would need four edits. Move the completion subcommand (with its long_about templated on the binary name) into `swissarmyhammer_cli_completions` and reuse it; the only per-binary variant is the program name in the examples.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:191` — install_target_arg is a verbatim third copy of an identical helper in two sibling binaries; the project/local/user target arg is now maintained in three places and will drift. Hoist a single `install_target_arg(help)` into a shared CLI-scaffold crate (alongside `swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen` / `swissarmyhammer_cli_completions`) and call it from all three binaries.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:242` — target_value is a verbatim third copy of the same `get_one::<String>("target").unwrap_or("project")` extraction. Share one `target_value(matches)` in the scaffold crate.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:254` — target_to_scope reimplements a target-string→InitScope mapping that already exists in three other places, including a typed `InitScope::from(InstallTarget)` in avp-cli — the canonical conversion should be reused rather than re-spelled as a string match that panics on the fourth arm. Reuse a single shared `&str -> InitScope` (or `InstallTarget -> InitScope`) conversion; prefer the typed `From` already defined in avp-cli rather than a stringly-typed match replicated per binary.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:320` — run_completion is a verbatim third copy of the same shell-extraction + print_completion dispatch in the sibling binaries. Hoist a shared `run_completion(matches, build_cli_fn, schema)` (or fold it into `print_completion`) into the completions crate.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:69` — The lifecycle subcommand builders `serve_subcommand`/`init_subcommand`/`deinit_subcommand`/`doctor_subcommand` plus the `build_cli` assembly are added as a third copy of the same scaffolding in kanban-cli and code-context-cli, differing only in about-strings. The whole 'schema ops + fixed lifecycle commands' tree shape is being maintained in parallel across three binaries. Add a shared lifecycle-command builder (parameterized by binary name + about-strings) to the cli_gen layer that already owns `build_commands_from_schema`, so each binary's `build_cli` = schema ops + shared lifecycle commands.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:301` — `run_completion` is a third near-identical copy of the completion-script writer in kanban-cli and code-context-cli — same body modulo the `Value` type alias. The completion-generation flow is duplicated rather than shared. Provide one generic `run_completion(matches, build_cli_fn)` (or take the assembled `Command`) in the shared CLI helper crate; each binary passes its own `build_cli`.

+### Nits

Minor for a private 3-call helper, but if it grows, distinguish the string roles (e.g. a small `FlagSpec { id, long, short, help }` builder) so id/long can't be swapped.
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-cli/src/main.rs:49` — The program-name literal "kanban" is repeated across the changed functions — `Command::new("kanban")` in build_cli, `lifecycle::completion_subcommand("kanban")`, and `lifecycle::run_completion(build_cli(schema), "kanban", sub_m)` in dispatch — three copies that must be renamed in lockstep if the binary is ever renamed. Name it once, e.g. `const PROG: &str = "kanban";` (or reuse `env!("CARGO_BIN_NAME")`), and reference that constant in all three call sites.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/tests/cli.rs:20` — The doc comment on `help_lists_schema_driven_operation_nouns` says the test asserts `--help` lists "every top-level subcommand declared in `cli::Commands`", but the body asserts schema-driven operation nouns (`command`, `processes`, `process`, `history`, `lines`) generated at runtime from the tool schema — not the `cli::Commands` enum. The comment describes the pre-refactor behavior and now misleads a reader about what the test guards. Update the doc comment to describe the schema-driven nouns it actually checks, e.g. "must exit successfully and list every schema-derived operation noun built from the shell tool schema.".
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/main.rs:1` — build_tool_arguments returns Result<serde_json::Map<...>, String> and stringifies failures via format!("Error processing arguments: {}", e). The error-handling rule says application code should use anyhow::Result<T>; a String error discards the source chain and the typed cause from extract_noun_verb_arguments / convert_matches_to_arguments. Return anyhow::Result and use `.context("processing tool arguments")` so the underlying error is preserved in the chain. If matching the surrounding CLI convention is intentional, leave as-is — this is consistent with the existing sibling code and is the reason for nit (not warning) severity.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/board/get.rs:27` — `fn include_counts(&self) -> bool { self.include_counts.unwrap_or(true) }` is a helper with a single call site (`if !self.include_counts()` in `execute`) wrapping a non-confusing expression — indirection without a second caller. Inline as `self.include_counts.unwrap_or(true)` at the call site, or keep it only if you consider documenting the default-true semantic worth the indirection (borderline — fine to leave).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/board/get.rs:30` — The private accessor `include_counts(&self) -> bool` shares its exact name with the public field `include_counts: Option<bool>`, so `self.include_counts` (the raw Option) and `self.include_counts()` (the resolved bool) differ only by a `()`.
